evidence.note: write_pre_registration records models, corpus_sha256, sequestered_split, judge_panel, seed, temperatures — all five fields — before the first call.
canonical_corpus_hash() hashes the sorted, canonically serialized whole scenario set (sha256 over json.dumps(sorted(...), sort_keys=True)) — a dropped or reordered scenario changes the digest.
test_prereg_written_before_first_call stubs the first call and asserts pre_registration.json exists on disk at that moment — the ordering is guarded by a test.
correction_policy: append-only in the prereg, run_id + timestamp make each run self-describing, and docs/methodology.md states the no-silent-retraction policy.
assert_author_allowed is a hard assert called in make_scenario; a scenario whose author is a contestant fails generation.
family_key() normalizes author and contestants to a vendor+base key before the membership check, so a sibling snapshot (e.g. acme-tool-1-0301) is also barred.
Every scenario carries an immutable author_model stamped at generation.
provenance is 'synthetic' on every scenario; a synthetic corpus needs no scraped-source contamination check.

CONCEPT SEARCH: the holdout is named the 'sequestered split' here — a grep for 'holdout' misses it. The split is run and scripts/publish.py::write_gap_table publishes a per-model public-vs-sequestered gap as the overfitting tripwire.
CONCEPT SEARCH: 'sequestered split' = holdout. artifacts/, transcripts/, traces/, .checkpoint.json, and data/sequestered/ are gitignored with a comment naming the split-leak reason.
An explicit 'Refuse to run with the sequestered split configured' step exits 1 if ACME_SEQUESTERED_DIR is set in public CI; no artifact upload is present.
sequestered_block() returns {sha256, n_scenarios} only — no scenario IDs, no per-scenario index.
The published board carries per-model {score, ci_low, ci_high} and band membership only; the gap table is per-model aggregates. No split scenario IDs, text, or per-scenario scores are published.
Scenarios are referenced by id in rows and stubs; no error path or log echoes scenario prompt text or ground truth. Doc examples are synthetic.
call_judge returns resolved_model, persisted per judge in score_transcript and per row in eval/run.py.
krippendorff_alpha() computes a chance-corrected inter-judge reliability (1 - Do/De), reported per docs/methodology.md — not a raw within-X rate.
acme-judge-1 shares the acme lab with contestant acme-tool-1; same_lab_recompute() reports the ranking with acme judges dropped. Guarded conceptually by the same-lab table in scripts/publish.py.
length_bias_slope() runs an OLS of score on answer_len; answer_len is recorded per row in eval/run.py.
RUBRIC_CRITERIA (correctness, efficiency, safety) are scored independently per judge and aggregated after scoring — atomic, not one holistic score.
The judging and statistics claims (atomic rubric, Krippendorff alpha, macro aggregation, fixed-seed scenario bootstrap, pass^k) each trace accurately to eval/scoring.py and eval/aggregate.py.
bootstrap_ci() is a fixed-seed (1234) bootstrap; the board publishes ci_low/ci_high per model.
bootstrap_ci resamples scenario ids, not correlated per-run rows.
aggregate() computes macro (mean of per-scenario means); docs/methodology.md states 'aggregation is macro'.
pass_hat_k() computes pass^k (all k runs succeed); docs/methodology.md defines it and distinguishes it from pass@k.
random.seed(args.seed) set and recorded in the pre-registration; the temperature-0 non-bit-for-bit caveat is stated in README and methodology.
rank_bands() clusters models whose CIs overlap into the same band instead of publishing a strict order — the multiple-comparison guard.
A single documented run sequence with inputs pinned to data/public/, plus an explicit 'reproducible for pinned corpus and seed; bounded not bit-for-bit across provider snapshots' caveat.
load_completed/mark_completed checkpoint completed (model, scenario) keys (resume), and assert_within_budget enforces a cost cap.
requires-python == 3.11.* with == dependency pins and a committed uv.lock; setup-python pinned to 3.11 in CI.
exclude_non_contestants drops NULL_AGENT; tests/test_integrity.py::test_null_agent_excluded is the tripwire.
exclude_non_contestants filters is_sequestered rows before the public aggregate; test_sequestered_rows_excluded guards it.
exclude_non_contestants keeps only config == 'default'; test_non_default_config_excluded guards it.
Three tripwire tests (null, sequestered, non-default) each fail if the excluded row reaches the board.
assert_complete() raises SystemExit when an expected model is missing; test_completeness_gate_blocks_partial_board guards it.
assert_no_publish_path_gitignored runs git check-ignore over every PUBLISH_PATH before git add; test_no_publish_path_gitignored and a CI step both enforce it.

The three doc-promised surfaces (leaderboard.json, gap_table.json, same_lab_table.json) are each written by a pipeline step and committed by the publish step; docs/methodology.md names exactly these three.
